note: Composition-pinning ledger for WIN-FND-007. It pins the CURRENT, real Windows icon-theme selection / package-naming / fallback contract and enumerates icon-themes/ to record an honest material_theme_installed:false. No Material icon source or assets exist yet, so there is nothing to guard -- this only fail-closed pins what is real today (the hardcoded Windows colibre fallback, the generic mPreferredIconTheme override that lets any correctly-named installed theme win, the images_<id>[_dark][_svg].zip naming any future material package must satisfy, the literal 20-directory icon-themes/ set with no material* directory, and the four qualitative iconography prose rules) so future drift is caught. Source/text evidence only; runtime_verified:false; no build, icon pixels, RTL-mirroring, or scaling evidence is claimed and no gate is moved.

windows_route.note: On _WIN32, GetIconThemeForDesktopEnvironment routes to the colibre / colibre_dark ids with no per-desktop routing (the elementary/breeze/sukapura desktop branches are #else only).

package_naming.note: Any future material theme must ship as images_material[_dark][_svg].zip to be discovered at all; the _svg/_dark suffixes are stripped for the display name.

rules.substring: RTL mirroring is applied per glyph
generated art is not accepted as runtime or evidence
rules.rule: no unreviewed generated art as evidence

rules.substring: theme for the native pipeline is specified here, not yet implemented
mirroring is semantic, not automatic for every glyph
Generated art is not accepted as

icon_size_linter.note: A size linter for lc_/sc_ PNGs exists and walks the whole icon-themes tree (so it would cover any future material PNGs at 24/16px), but it is one of the stock upstream linters excluded from the Material validator gate: the script exists, CI does not currently enforce it in the Windows UI contract workflow.
